name: rung_290_story_multi_branch_pronoun_pressure
utterances: Treat this like a crowded family chapter with pronouns that keep trying to slide onto the wrong branch.
assert rule ancestor(X, Y) :- parent(X, Y).
assert rule ancestor(X, Z) :- parent(X, Y), ancestor(Y, Z).
assert rule in_region(X, Z) :- at(X, Y), inside(Y, Z).

Family chunk from the quay chapters: assert fact parent(orla, jalen). assert fact parent(orla, mira). assert fact parent(jalen, sena). assert fact parent(mira, taren). assert fact parent(mira, elowen).
The location and prop lines are split across the next page: assert fact at(taren, lantern_house). assert fact inside(lantern_house, low_harbor). assert fact carries(mira, brass_key).
Quick continuity check before the copy editor intervenes: query ancestor(orla, taren). Query ancestor(orla, sena). Query carries(mira, brass_key).
Copy-edit correction: the sentence that says 'her son waited behind him' was attached to the wrong sibling. retract fact parent(mira, taren).
Copy-edit continuation, explicit form: assert fact parent(jalen, taren).
Pronoun resolution note from the margin: when it says 'she left the brass key with him,' she is Mira and him is Jalen. retract fact carries(mira, brass_key).
One last staging fix for the same scene: retract fact at(taren, lantern_house).
Staging continuation, explicit form: assert fact at(taren, silt_stairs). assert fact inside(silt_stairs, low_harbor).
After those fixes, answer from the corrected branch only: query parent(mira, elowen). Query parent(jalen, taren). Query in_region(taren, low_harbor).
